pages.name: Home
pages.description: Entry point for the decluttering and organizing service, highlighting key services, packages, and resources.
primary_functions: Introduce the brand, core services, and value proposition.
Provide a primary “Book Now” call-to-action that leads to the service selection/booking flow (e.g., services.html or booking.html with default service selector).
Provide quick links to Packages, Virtual Services, Shop Products, In-Home Assessment, Resources, and Gift Cards.
Show featured packages, products, and recent blog articles to guide users into relevant flows.

params.serviceId: <derived from primary_cta.default_context, e.g., default home office service ID>

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the primary “Book Now” button in the main hero banner.
Click a featured service section CTA such as “Explore Services”.
Click a featured package CTA such as “View Whole Home Packages”.
Click a featured product tile or “Shop Closet Storage” link.
Click a “Virtual Coaching” or similar quick link.
Click an in-banner or section CTA labeled “In-Home Assessment”.
Click a featured article card in the recent articles section.
Click a “Give a Gift Card” CTA in the homepage content.
Click the cart icon in the header from the homepage.

access_methods.description: Default landing page and accessible via the “Home” link in the header navigation.
Users can directly open index.html via the site root URL.

pages.name: Services
pages.description: Catalog of in-person organizing services and service-based packages, organized by category.
primary_functions: List service categories such as “Home Office Decluttering” and “Closet & Wardrobe Organization” with filters (e.g., categoryId=home_office, categoryId=closet_wardrobe_organization).
Within a selected category, display service and package cards with key attributes (total hours, price, customer rating).
Provide filters for services/packages (e.g., total hours >=10, max price, rating) and sorting (e.g., rating high to low).
Link each service or package card to either the booking flow (booking.html?serviceId=home_office_decluttering) or detailed configuration (package_detail.html?packageId=closet_refresh).

incoming_params.description: Optional ServiceCategory ID (e.g., 'home_office', 'closet_wardrobe_organization') used to pre-select a service category when the page loads.
Optional PackageCategory ID used to filter package cards associated with a service category.
Optional filter for minimum total hours when deep-linking to filtered service/package lists.
Optional maximum price filter applied on initial load if present.
Optional minimum rating filter applied on initial load if present.
Optional sort preset (e.g., 'rating_desc', 'price_asc') applied when linking directly to a sorted view.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the “Book Now” button on a service card such as “Home Office Decluttering”.
Click a package card or its “View Details” action for a service-based package (e.g., a closet or multi-session package).
Click a shortcut “Book This Package” action on a package card, if provided.
Click the cart icon in the header while browsing services.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the “Services” link in the main header navigation.
Linked from homepage sections, About page, and other content CTAs that promote specific services.
Filters and selected category can be preconfigured using query parameters such as serviceCategoryId.

pages.name: Booking
pages.description: Unified booking flow for one-off services and bookable packages, including schedule and customer details.
primary_functions: Display the selected service or package (e.g., via serviceId=home_office_decluttering or packageId=closet_wardrobe_10hr) and allow choosing session options such as duration (e.g., “2-hour session”).
Provide a date picker to select a desired appointment date (e.g., Wednesday, September 18).
Offer time window filters (e.g., 2:00 pm–5:00 pm, or Saturday mornings) and a sorting dropdown (e.g., “Price: Low to High”) for available time slots.
Show slot options with visible total prices so users can pick a slot under their budget (e.g., under $200).
Present a booking details form to capture name, email, phone, payment option (e.g., “Pay at appointment”), and a “Confirm Booking” button to finalize the appointment.

incoming_params.description: ID of a Service to book (e.g., the Home Office Decluttering service) when entering the booking flow.
ID of a Package to book (e.g., a closet organization package) when booking a multi-hour bundle.
Optional pre-selected SessionOption (e.g., 2-hour vs 3-hour) if chosen before arriving at the booking page.
Optional pre-selected appointment date (ISO 8601 date string, e.g., '2025-09-18').
Optional filter for the start of a preferred time window (e.g., '14:00').
Optional filter for the end of a preferred time window (e.g., '17:00').
Optional budget ceiling used when initially loading available slots, if provided.
Optional sort preset for slots (e.g., 'price_asc').

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the cart icon in the header from within the booking flow (view existing cart items if any).

access_methods.description: Accessed when a serviceId or packageId is provided in the query string from Services, Package Detail, or the homepage CTA.
Reached via in-page “Book Now” or “Book This Package” actions from Services and Package Detail pages.

pages.name: Packages
pages.description: Overview of curated organizing packages, especially whole-home and multi-room bundles.
primary_functions: List available packages grouped by type, including a category filter for options like “Whole Home” (e.g., categoryId=whole_home).
Provide basic filters (e.g., price range, approximate room count) and sorting to help users find an appropriate package.
Display package cards (e.g., “Whole Home Refresh”) with summary info: included rooms, hours, and starting price.
Allow users to click a package card to view and customize its full details on package_detail.html.

incoming_params.description: Optional PackageCategory ID (e.g., 'whole_home') to pre-select a package category such as Whole Home bundles.
Optional minimum bedroom count filter applied when deep-linking to a tailored set of packages.
Optional maximum bedroom count filter applied on initial page load if present.
Optional minimum package price filter used for preconfigured views.
Optional maximum package price filter used for preconfigured views.
Optional sorting preset such as 'price_asc' or 'rating_desc'.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a package card such as “Whole Home Refresh” to configure bedrooms and add-ons.
Click the cart icon in the header while viewing packages.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the “Packages” link in the main header navigation.
Linked from homepage and About page sections that highlight Whole Home or bundled offers.
Pre-filtered package views can be shared/bookmarked using query parameters like packageCategoryId and price range.

pages.name: Package Detail
pages.description: Configuration page for organizing packages and virtual bundles, with add-ons and live pricing.
primary_functions: Show comprehensive details of a selected package or virtual coaching bundle (via packageId=whole_home_refresh, packageId=virtual_coaching_3x60).
Provide room and option configuration controls (e.g., set number of bedrooms to 3 using dropdown or plus/minus).
Offer an “Add-On Services” section with filters (e.g., price <= $50) and allow selecting multiple add-ons while showing their combined cost (e.g., ensure two add-ons total < $80).
Display a summary panel showing configured options (e.g., “3 bedrooms” and exactly two add-ons) and updated total price.
Enable actions such as “Update Package”/“Save Customization”, “Add Package to Cart” for cart checkout flows, and “Book This Package” to start a scheduling flow in booking.html.

incoming_params.description: ID of the Package or virtual coaching bundle to display and configure.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the “Add Package to Cart” button after configuring bedrooms and add-ons.
Click the “Book This Package” button to move into the scheduling flow for the configured package.

access_methods.description: Accessed with a packageId query parameter when a user selects a package from Packages, Services, or Virtual Services listings.
Reached by clicking a “View Details” or similar CTA on a package card.

pages.name: Shop Products
pages.description: Product listing page for physical organizing products and storage solutions.
primary_functions: Show product categories (e.g., “Closet & Wardrobe Storage”) and allow filtering by category (categoryId=closet_wardrobe_storage).

Provide filters for price range (e.g., 15–30), customer rating (e.g., 4 stars & up), capacity/size (e.g., 20L or larger), color/material (e.g., variant “Clear”), and shipping options (e.g., “Free Shipping”).
Allow sorting of results (e.g., by price, rating) and display product cards that reflect the applied filters.
Let users click a product card to open its details on product_detail.html for more information and purchasing.

incoming_params.description: Optional ProductCategory ID (e.g., 'closet_wardrobe_storage') used to pre-select a product category view.
Optional minimum price filter for preconfigured or shared filtered views.
Optional maximum price filter for preconfigured or shared filtered views.
Optional minimum rating filter, e.g., 4 for '4 stars & up'.
Optional minimum capacity in liters (e.g., 20) for size-based deep links.
Optional color filter (e.g., 'Clear') used in bookmarked filtered views.
Optional style filter applied on initial load if provided.
Optional flag to pre-filter results to items with free shipping.
Optional sorting preset such as 'price_asc' used for shared search URLs.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a product card such as a set of clear storage bins to view full details.
Click the cart icon in the header after adding items.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the “Shop Products” link in the header navigation.
Linked from homepage featured products and from relevant blog/resource content.
Supports deep-linked filtered searches via query parameters such as categoryId and price range.

pages.name: Product Detail
pages.description: Detail page for a single physical product such as storage bins or organizing tools.
primary_functions: Display product title, images, description, key specifications (capacity in liters, dimensions), rating, and shipping details (including whether shipping is free).
Show available variants like color/material (e.g., “Clear”) and ensure the selected variant matches the filters chosen on shop.html.
Allow the user to set quantity (e.g., 4 clear storage bins) and see the line item subtotal.
Provide an “Add to Cart” button that adds the configured product and quantity to cart.html.

incoming_params.description: ID of the Product whose details should be displayed.
Optional ID of a specific ProductVariant to pre-select (e.g., the 'Clear' variant).

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the “Add to Cart” button after selecting quantity and variant (e.g., quantity 4 clear bins).

access_methods.description: Accessed via a productId (and optional variantId) parameter from the Shop Products listing or other product links.
Reached when clicking a featured product tile on the homepage or in related blog content.

pages.name: Virtual Services
pages.description: Listing page dedicated to virtual offerings, especially decluttering coaching bundles.
primary_functions: Present virtual service categories (e.g., categoryId=virtual_decluttering_coaching) and allow users to select the “Virtual Decluttering Coaching” category.
Provide filters for “Number of Sessions” (e.g., 3 sessions), “Session Length” (e.g., 60-minute sessions), and price range as needed.
Offer sorting options such as “Price: Low to High” to surface the least expensive qualifying bundle first.
Display bundle cards that link to detailed configuration/purchase pages on package_detail.html, where users can add a bundle to the cart.

incoming_params.description: Optional virtual ServiceCategory or PackageCategory identifier, typically 'virtual_decluttering_coaching', to pre-select the virtual coaching view.
Optional filter to pre-select bundles by number of sessions (e.g., 3).
Optional filter to pre-select bundles by session length (e.g., 60).
Optional minimum price filter for deep-linked virtual coaching searches.
Optional maximum price filter for deep-linked virtual coaching searches.
Optional sorting preset (e.g., 'price_asc') for virtual bundles.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the first 3-session, 60-minute virtual decluttering coaching bundle after filtering and sorting by price.
Click the cart icon in the header while exploring virtual services.

access_methods.description: Accessible from the “Virtual Services” link in the main header navigation.
Linked from homepage virtual coaching promos and related blog content.
Supports deep links to pre-filtered bundles by sessions, length, and price.

pages.name: In-Home Assessment
pages.description: Information and request form for an in-home assessment visit.
primary_functions: Explain what an in-home assessment includes, typical duration, and how it supports larger organizing projects.
Provide a prominent “Request Assessment” button that reveals or scrolls to the inquiry form on the same page.

Offer a form to capture name, email, phone, address/city (e.g., “Downtown District”), home type (e.g., Apartment), number of bedrooms (e.g., 2), square footage (e.g., 900), and preferred day/time (e.g., Wednesday, Evening 5–8 pm).
Include an “Additional Details/Notes” textarea for budget and project details (e.g., budget under $400) and a “Submit Request” button to send the inquiry.

incoming_params.description: Optional source indicator (e.g., 'homepage', 'services') for analytics or copy variations; does not change core behavior.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the cart icon in the header while viewing the assessment page.

access_methods.description: Accessed via the “In-Home Assessment” link in the main header navigation.
Linked from homepage CTAs encouraging users to start with an in-home assessment.

pages.name: Blog & Resources
pages.description: Content hub for articles, guides, and resource posts related to organizing and decluttering.
primary_functions: List blog articles with category and tag filters (e.g., categoryId=kitchen_organization and tagId=small_kitchens).
Allow date-based filtering and sorting (e.g., “Last 6 Months” or “Newest First”).
Highlight small-kitchen organization content so the first relevant article about small kitchens is easily accessible.
Provide links from each article preview to the full article on article.html.

incoming_params.description: Optional BlogCategory ID (e.g., 'kitchen_organization') to pre-filter posts by category.
Optional Tag ID (e.g., 'small_kitchens') to further refine the article listing.
Optional date filter preset (e.g., 'last_6_months') to constrain articles by recency.
Optional sorting preset, commonly 'newest_first'.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click an article card such as the newest small-kitchen organization article.
Click a “Shop Related Products” link from a resource block in the blog listing.
Click the cart icon in the header while browsing blog content.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the “Resources” link in the header navigation.
Accessible via the “Resources / Blog” link in the footer navigation.
Linked from homepage featured articles and other internal content.
Supports pre-filtered views of articles based on category, tag, and date presets.

pages.name: Article Detail
pages.description: Full content view for a single blog article, with related downloads and bookmarking.
primary_functions: Display the full article content including images and formatting for topics like small-kitchen organization.
Provide a “Save to Reading List” or “Bookmark” action that stores the article in a local reading list for the single user.

Show a “Related Resources” or “Downloads” section that lists associated resources, including any free printable checklists (e.g., “Kitchen Decluttering Checklist”) with clear price labels ($0 or Free).
Link each related resource to its own detail page on download_detail.html for more information and the ability to add it to the cart.

incoming_params.description: ID of the Article to display in full detail.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a related resource such as a free “Kitchen Decluttering Checklist” in the Related Resources section.
Use a breadcrumb or “Back to Resources” link from the article page.
Click the cart icon in the header while reading an article.

access_methods.description: Accessed via articleId when a user selects an article from the blog listing or reading list.
Linked from the homepage recent articles section and from internal promotions pointing to specific articles.

pages.name: Download Detail
pages.description: Detail page for digital or printable resources such as checklists and guides.
primary_functions: Show title, description, preview (if applicable), and pricing for a downloadable resource (e.g., a checklist priced at $0 / Free).
Clarify the delivery method (e.g., downloadable PDF after checkout) and any usage notes.
Provide an “Add to Cart” button that adds the free or paid download to cart.html like any other item.
Support use as a target for related-resource links from article.html (e.g., downloadId=kitchen_decluttering_checklist).

incoming_params.description: ID of the DownloadableResource (e.g., a checklist or guide) to display.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the “Add to Cart” button to add a free or paid downloadable checklist to the cart.
Click a link back to a related article from the download detail page, if provided.

access_methods.description: Accessed with a downloadId parameter from Related Resources sections or other download listings.
Linked directly from articles or promotional blocks highlighting specific downloads.

pages.name: Gift Cards
pages.description: Configuration and purchase page for digital and physical gift cards.
primary_functions: Offer selection between “Digital Gift Card” and “Physical Gift Card,” with the ability to choose the physical option for mailed cards.
Allow users to select or enter a custom amount (e.g., $150) for the gift card value.
Display available card designs and let users choose a neutral/minimal design option.
Provide fields for occasion (e.g., Birthday), recipient name (e.g., Sam Lee), and sender name (e.g., Pat Jordan).
Add the configured gift card to cart.html via “Add to Cart” or continue to cart, where shipping options (including a standard shipping method under $8) will be selected.

incoming_params.description: Optional type of gift card to pre-select, such as 'physical' or 'digital'.
Optional preset amount to pre-populate the gift card value.
Optional design style hint (e.g., 'neutral', 'minimal') for pre-selecting a design.
Optional preset occasion (e.g., 'birthday') when linking from a contextual promotion.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the “Add to Cart” or “Continue” button after choosing gift card amount, neutral design, and recipient/sender details.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the “Gift Cards” link in the header navigation.
Accessible via the “Gift Cards” link in the footer navigation.
Linked from homepage gifting sections or other promotional content.
Supports pre-selecting type and amount for campaign-driven gift card URLs.

pages.name: Cart
pages.description: Shopping cart for managing all items prior to checkout, including products, packages, downloads, and gift cards.
primary_functions: Display all items in the cart (physical products, packages, virtual bundles, gift cards, digital downloads) with quantities, individual prices, and subtotals.
Allow updating quantities or removing items from the cart.
For physical items such as products and physical gift cards, present shipping method choices (including a Standard Shipping option under $8) and update order totals accordingly.
Show cart subtotal, shipping, and estimated total, with a “Proceed to Checkout” button that leads to checkout.html.

incoming_params.description: Optional cart_item_id to visually highlight the most recently added item.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the “Proceed to Checkout” button after reviewing cart items and shipping selections.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the “Cart” icon/link in the header navigation from any page.
Accessible via the “Cart” link in the footer navigation.
Automatically directed here after adding products, packages, downloads, or gift cards to the cart when the user chooses to view the cart.
Optional highlightItemId parameter supports focusing on a recently added item.

pages.name: Checkout
pages.description: Order information and confirmation step before entering payment details.
primary_functions: Summarize all items in the order, including packages, virtual services, products, downloads, and any gift cards, along with selected shipping options from cart.html.
Provide fields to capture customer contact details (e.g., name and email such as Jordan Kim, jordan.kim@example.com).
Offer a promo code field where users can enter and apply a code (e.g., “TIDY10”) and see the updated total.
Provide a “Continue to Payment” button that transitions the user to payment.html to complete the transaction.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the “Continue to Payment” button after entering contact information and (optionally) applying promo code TIDY10.
Click a “Back to Cart” link or breadcrumb to adjust items before finalizing checkout.

access_methods.description: Accessed after the user clicks “Proceed to Checkout” from cart.html; not directly linked in primary navigation.

pages.name: Payment
pages.description: Final payment step to complete purchases for services, products, and gift cards.
primary_functions: Display final order summary, including any discounts (e.g., from promo code TIDY10) and confirmed shipping charges.
Allow the user to select a payment method (e.g., credit card, digital wallet) and enter required payment details.
Handle special cases where payment is deferred (e.g., if “Pay at appointment” was chosen in booking.html, this page can simply confirm no online payment is needed).
Provide a “Place Order” or equivalent confirmation button to complete the purchase and show a confirmation message or redirect to a confirmation view.

incoming_params.description: ID of the Order created at the end of checkout, used to load final payment summary.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Optionally redirect back to the homepage with a success banner after successful payment.

access_methods.description: Accessed with an orderId parameter from checkout.html after starting checkout.
Displayed as the next step after the user clicks “Continue to Payment” on the checkout page.

pages.name: About
pages.description: Background information about the professional organizer, approach, and service area.
primary_functions: Describe the business story, professional qualifications, and organizing philosophy.
Outline the service areas covered (e.g., neighborhoods or cities) and the types of projects typically accepted.
Provide clear contact information (email, phone, possibly a simple contact section) for general inquiries.
Link back to key action pages such as services.html, packages.html, assessment.html, and gift_cards.html.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a CTA such as “Explore Services” from the About page.
Click a CTA such as “View Packages” from the About page.
Click “Request an In-Home Assessment” from the About page.
Click “Send a Gift Card” from the About page.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the “About” link in the footer navigation.
Can be linked from external profiles or directories describing the organizer.

pages.name: Policies
pages.description: Consolidated policy information covering privacy, terms, cancellations, and shipping.
primary_functions: Present privacy and data handling information relevant to form submissions and orders.
Outline booking, rescheduling, and cancellation policies for in-person and virtual services.
Describe shipping, delivery times, and return/refund policies for physical products and gift cards (including standard shipping options and costs).
Detail usage terms for digital downloads and gift cards.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a contextual link like “View Cart” mentioned in shipping/returns sections, if present.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the “Policies” link in the footer navigation.
Linked from emails or external pages when referencing privacy, booking, or shipping policies.

header_links.description: Homepage with primary overview and main calls-to-action.
Browse in-person decluttering and organizing services, including closet organization packages.
View and select curated organizing packages such as whole-home bundles.
Storefront for physical organizing products and storage solutions.
Browse virtual decluttering coaching and other remote services.
Access articles, guides, and downloadable resources on organization.
Learn about and request an in-home organizing assessment.
Purchase digital or physical gift cards for organizing services.
View items added for purchase and proceed to checkout.

footer_links.description: Learn more about the organizer, service areas, and contact information.
Overview of available decluttering and organizing services.
Details on bundled organizing packages and multi-room offers.
Browse and buy storage products and organizing tools.
Find virtual coaching and remote organizing options.
Read organizing tips and access checklists and other resources.
Request an in-home assessment for your space.
Send organizing gift cards to friends and family.
View privacy, terms, cancellation, shipping, and refund policies.
Review your cart before checking out.
